children.label: i
children.text: significantly fail to implement or enforce regulations to inspect ships, aircraft, cargo, or conveyances in transit to or from North Korea, as required by applicable United Nations Security Council resolutions;

children.label: ii
children.text: facilitate the transfer, transshipment, or conveyance of significant types or quantities of cargo, vessels, or aircraft owned or controlled by persons designated under applicable United Nations Security Council resolutions; or

children.label: iii
children.text: facilitate any of the activities described in ;section 9214(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: describing the extent to which the requirements of applicable United Nations Security Council resolutions to de-register any vessel owned, controlled, or operated by or on behalf of the Government of North Korea have been implemented by other foreign countries;

children.label: C
children.text: describing the compliance of the Islamic Republic of Iran with the sanctions mandated in applicable United Nations Security Council resolutions;

children.label: D
children.text: identifying vessels, aircraft, and conveyances owned or controlled by the Reconnaissance General Bureau of the Workers’ Party of Korea; and

children.label: E
children.text: describing the diplomatic and enforcement efforts by the President to secure the full implementation of the applicable United Nations Security Council resolutions, as described in subparagraphs (A) through (C).

children.heading: Form
children.label: 2 Form
children.text: The report required under paragraph (1) shall be submitted in unclassified form but may contain a classified annex.
The ports of Dandong, Dalian, and any other port in the People’s Republic of China that the President deems appropriate.

The ports of Abadan, Bandar-e-Abbas, Chabahar, Bandar-e-Khomeini, Bushehr Port, Asaluyeh Port, Kish, Kharg Island, Bandar-e-Lenge, and Khorramshahr, and Tehran Imam Khomeini International Airport, in the Islamic Republic of Iran.
The ports of Nakhodka, Vanino, and Vladivostok, in the Russian Federation.
The ports of Latakia, Banias, and Tartous, and Damascus International Airport, in the Syrian Arab Republic.

children.label: A
children.text: has been transported through a sea port or airport the operator of which has been identified by the President in accordance with subsection (a)(1) as having repeatedly failed to comply with applicable United Nations Security Council resolutions;

children.label: B
children.text: is aboard a vessel or aircraft, or within a conveyance that has, within the last 365 days, entered the territory or waters of North Korea, or landed in any of the sea ports or airports of North Korea; or

children.label: C
children.text: is registered by a country or jurisdiction whose compliance has been identified by the President as deficient pursuant to subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Exception for food, medicine, and humanitarian shipments
children.label: 2 Exception for food, medicine, and humanitarian shipments
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any vessel, aircraft, or conveyance that has entered the territory or waters of North Korea, or landed in any of the sea ports or airports of North Korea, exclusively for the purposes described in , or to import food, medicine, or supplies into North Korea to meet the humanitarian needs of the North Korean people.section 9228(b)(3)(B) of this title
part V of title IV of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( et seq.).19 U.S.C. 1581
